summary: Gruber announces that Markdown won third place in Six Apart's Plug In To Movable Type 3.0 Developer's Contest. His prizes included a 40 GB iPod, a $100 iTunes gift certificate, and Adobe Creative Suite Premium. He notes Jay Allen won the grand prize for MT-Blacklist. Unable to attend the BlogOn conference where awards were announced, Gruber wrote an absurdly grandiose acceptance speech comparing his plug-in award to the 1980 U.S. Olympic hockey team's Miracle on Ice.
key_points: Markdown placed third in Six Apart's MT 3.0 developer contest, an early formal recognition of the tool
Jay Allen's MT-Blacklist, a comment-spam blocker, won the grand prize — reflecting the era's blog infrastructure priorities
Gruber's mock acceptance speech hilariously equates a plug-in contest win with the Miracle on Ice, perfectly capturing his deadpan humor
money_quotes: A young, rag-tag team of American hockey players — more boys than men, really — faced off against the quote-unquote "unbeatable" Soviet Red Army.
With this award for Markdown, my Movable Type plug-in, my answer to that question is "Yes". Thank you.
key_insight: Gruber celebrates Markdown's early contest win with a comically overblown acceptance speech that makes winning third place for a text-formatting plug-in sound like the greatest upset in sports history.
